name: Software Architect
role: Designs the overall UE5 system architecture including gameplay framework, replication graph, and server-authoritative combat validation — the foundational decisions everything else depends on

name: Game Designer
role: Owns the GDD-level systems design for Sekiro-style parry mechanics, class differentiation, and the economy balancing for Battle Pass progression that directly drives engineering requirements

name: Studio Producer
role: Manages the multi-discipline roadmap across UE5 client, AWS backend, and multi-platform CI/CD builds — scope too large and cross-functional for a standard PM

name: Performance Benchmarker
role: Establishes and enforces the 60 FPS rendering budget and server-frame-time baselines for Nanite/Lumen under arena load — critical QA gate before each milestone

name: Technical Artist
role: Bridges the art-to-engine pipeline for Motion Matching rigs, Niagara hit/parry/deflect VFX, and Nanite LOD budgeting — directly owns the most performance-sensitive visual systems in UE5

name: Game Audio Engineer
role: Specializes in adaptive audio systems across all game engines, making them the right fit for MetaSounds graph design and spatial audio budgeting in a fast-paced arena combat context

name: Backend Architect
role: Designs the scalable server-side architecture spanning GameLift fleet orchestration, Redis session state, and the FastAPI/.NET microservices for player progression and the cosmetics shop

name: Database Optimizer
role: Owns PostgreSQL schema design, indexing strategy, and query optimization for high-write match history and leaderboard queries under concurrent arena load

name: DevOps Automator
role: Builds and maintains the multi-platform UE5 build pipeline targeting Windows, macOS, and PlayStation consoles alongside automated GameLift fleet deployment — a non-trivial CI/CD surface

name: Security Engineer
role: Hardens the authoritative server against speed hacks, hit-registration exploits, and packet manipulation — essential for a competitive arena game where cheating directly breaks the core experience

name: UI Designer
role: Designs the pixel-perfect HUD component system in UMG and the cosmetics shop interface, ensuring combat-critical information is readable at 60 FPS under high-intensity arena conditions
